tasks.description: Create a standardized directory structure for exported policies that vtol-interface can discover by task type.

steps.description: Define the export root directory as ~/policy_exports
Create subdirectories for each task type: hover, nav, track, collision_avoidance
Document the mapping between experiment names and task directories

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Export directory structure exists with task subdirectories. Each task directory can contain model.onnx and observation_metadata.yaml files. Documentation clearly maps experiment names to task directories.

unit: Standardized export directory structure correctly maps experiment names like 'vtol_hover' to 'hover' directory
Task directories include all supported task types: hover, nav, track, collision_avoidance

tasks.description: Add automatic ONNX export functionality to the training runner that triggers when training completes.

steps.description: Create a post-training export function that integrates with the RSL-RL runner
Implement task detection logic from experiment name to determine export directory
Integrate export hook into the main training loop completion

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Training automatically exports latest checkpoint to ONNX format when complete. Export function correctly identifies task type from experiment name. ONNX model and metadata are saved to the correct task directory.

unit: Post-training export function correctly identifies task from experiment name patterns like 'vtol_hover' -> 'hover'
Export function handles unknown experiment names gracefully with appropriate error handling
Export function creates ONNX model with correct input/output shapes for different tasks
integration: Post-training export hook integrates with existing RSL-RL training runner without breaking existing functionality
Training completion triggers automatic export to correct task directory

tasks.description: Integrate observation metadata extraction with the trained model environment to create complete metadata files.

steps.description: Modify the export pipeline to extract observation metadata from the actual trained environment
Ensure metadata includes both model info and observation feature definitions in single file
Add task-specific metadata fields including task name and policy version

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Observation metadata YAML includes complete feature definitions with names and dimensions. Metadata contains task identification and policy version information. Export works with actual trained model environments.

unit: Enhanced metadata exporter produces YAML with both model metadata and observation features
Task-specific metadata includes correct task name derived from environment configuration
Observation feature extraction works with actual IsaacLab environment configurations
integration: Enhanced metadata exporter integrates with post-training export pipeline
Combined metadata file contains all information needed by vtol-interface

tasks.description: Create a system that maintains only the latest policy for each task and handles versioning.

steps.description: Implement cleanup logic to remove old policy files before saving new ones
Add version tracking using timestamps or iteration numbers
Create backup mechanism for previous versions before overwriting

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Each task directory contains only the latest policy files. Previous versions are optionally backed up. Version information is included in metadata. Old files are cleaned up automatically.

unit: Latest policy management correctly removes old files before saving new ones
Version tracking includes meaningful identifiers like timestamps or iteration numbers
Backup mechanism preserves previous versions when enabled
integration: Latest policy management integrates with post-training export pipeline
vtol-interface can reliably discover the latest policy for each task

tasks.description: Update vtol-interface to use the standardized export directory for model discovery.

steps.description: Configure ModelDiscoverer search paths to include ~/policy_exports/
Verify compatibility with existing export format and metadata structure

tasks.acceptance-criteria: vtol-interface ModelDiscoverer can find models in ~/policy_exports/ directory. Task-specific discovery works correctly for all supported tasks. Backward compatibility maintained with existing deployment setup.

unit: Task-specific discovery finds models in task subdirectories like ~/policy_exports/hover/
integration: vtol-interface successfully loads and validates models from new export directory structure
End-to-end inference works with automatically exported policies
e2e-manual: Manual verification that vtol-interface can discover and load latest policies for different tasks

tasks.description: Develop comprehensive integration tests that validate the complete train-to-deploy pipeline.

steps.description: Create test that simulates complete training and export cycle
Implement validation of exported ONNX model and metadata
Test vtol-interface discovery and inference with exported artifacts

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Integration test covers complete pipeline from training through vtol-interface inference. Test validates ONNX model correctness, metadata completeness, and task-specific discovery. All tests pass consistently.

unit: Integration test suite covers all task types: hover, nav, track, collision_avoidance
integration: End-to-end test validates complete train-to-deploy pipeline functionality
Exported artifacts are validated for ONNX compliance and metadata correctness
e2e-manual: Manual verification that the complete pipeline works in real Docker training environment
